encounter.reason: Missed abortion diagnosed, expectant management counseling
encounter.location: Lone Star Women's Health, Austin, TX

screening.name: crown-rump length
screening.note: Ultrasound measurement consistent with gestational age; confirms intrauterine location

screening.name: fetal cardiac activity
screening.note: No fetal cardiac activity detected; confirms fetal demise (missed abortion)

screening.name: adnexal mass
screening.note: No adnexal mass; rules out ectopic pregnancy or other adnexal pathology

screening.name: pelvic free fluid
screening.note: No free fluid in pelvis; no peritoneal hemorrhage or other fluid collection

conditions.name: missed abortion
conditions.note: First trimester pregnancy loss; intrauterine gestation measuring 9 weeks 4 days with no fetal cardiac activity on transvaginal ultrasound

conditions.name: emotional distress related to pregnancy loss
conditions.note: Secondary to missed abortion diagnosis; patient tearful but appropriate; emotional support counseling provided

document_references.author: Dr. Carmen Villanueva
document_references.description: Office visit note documenting evaluation of first-trimester pregnancy loss, vital signs, transvaginal ultrasound findings confirming missed abortion, CBC results, assessment of missed abortion with clinical stability, counseling on management options (expectant, medical, surgical), patient's informed choice of expectant management, warning signs education, and follow-up plan for 1–2 weeks

document_references.author: Dr. Carmen Villanueva
document_references.description: Written discharge instructions detailing expected course with expectant management, anticipated vaginal bleeding and cramping over 1–2 weeks, warning signs requiring urgent/emergency evaluation (soaking more than 2 pads per hour, fever, severe pain, dizziness, syncope, foul-smelling discharge), activity recommendations, self-care guidance, and office contact information

diagnostic_reports.description: Transvaginal ultrasound demonstrating intrauterine pregnancy with crown-rump length consistent with 9 weeks 4 days gestation. No fetal cardiac activity detected, confirming fetal demise (missed abortion). No adnexal mass or free fluid identified. Findings rule out ectopic pregnancy, hemorrhagic complication, or infection; patient clinically stable
